segments.fr: Léa habite dans un petit village près de Tours.
segments.en: Léa lives in a small village near Tours.
segments.grammarNote: The verb 'habiter' takes no preposition before 'dans' when naming where someone lives.
segments.pronunciationTip: 'Tours' ends in a silent 's', it sounds like 'toor'.

segments.fr: Un matin, elle emprunte le vélo de son frère pour aller au travail.
segments.en: One morning, she borrows her brother's bike to go to work.
segments.grammarNote: 'Emprunter' means 'to borrow', not 'to lend' — that's 'prêter'.

segments.fr: Le vélo est trop grand pour elle, mais elle n'a pas le choix.
segments.en: The bike is too big for her, but she has no choice.
segments.grammarNote: 'Trop' before an adjective means 'too', showing excess.
segments.pronunciationTip: 'Choix' rhymes with English 'swa', the final 'x' is silent.

segments.fr: Sur la route, elle rencontre sa voisine qui rit en la voyant.
segments.en: On the road, she meets her neighbour, who laughs when she sees her.
segments.grammarNote: 'En + present participle' ('en la voyant') expresses 'while doing something'.

segments.fr: Léa arrive au travail en retard, mais de bonne humeur.
segments.en: Léa arrives at work late, but in a good mood.
segments.grammarNote: 'En retard' is the fixed phrase for 'late'; never 'tard' alone here.
segments.pronunciationTip: 'Humeur' has a nasal-free 'eu' sound, like the vowel in 'peur'.

segments.fr: Le soir, elle achète son propre vélo, à sa taille.
segments.en: In the evening, she buys her own bike, her size.
segments.grammarNote: 'Propre' before a noun means 'own'; after a noun it means 'clean'.

quiz.q: Why does Léa borrow her brother's bike?
What problem does Léa have with the bike?
choices: It is too big for her

quiz.q: Who does Léa meet on the road?
What does Léa do that evening?

segments.fr: Marc sort de chez lui sans regarder le ciel.
segments.en: Marc leaves his house without looking at the sky.
segments.grammarNote: 'Sans + infinitive' means 'without doing something'.
segments.pronunciationTip: 'Ciel' sounds like 'syel', the 'c' is soft before 'i'.

segments.fr: Après dix minutes de marche, il commence à pleuvoir très fort.
segments.en: After ten minutes of walking, it starts to rain very hard.
segments.grammarNote: 'Commencer à' + infinitive introduces the start of an action.

segments.fr: Marc n'a pas de parapluie, alors il court vers un café.
segments.en: Marc doesn't have an umbrella, so he runs toward a café.
segments.grammarNote: 'Alors' links a cause to its result, like 'so' in English.

segments.fr: À l'intérieur, il commande un chocolat chaud pour se réchauffer.
segments.en: Inside, he orders a hot chocolate to warm himself up.
segments.grammarNote: 'Se réchauffer' is reflexive: the action returns to the subject.

segments.fr: Une femme assise près de lui lui prête son parapluie.
segments.en: A woman sitting near him lends him her umbrella.
segments.grammarNote: 'Prêter' means 'to lend'; the opposite of 'emprunter'.

segments.fr: Marc la remercie chaleureusement et rentre chez lui au sec.
segments.en: Marc thanks her warmly and gets home dry.
segments.grammarNote: 'Au sec' is a fixed expression meaning 'staying dry'.

quiz.q: Why does Marc get caught in the rain?
choices: He left without checking the sky

quiz.q: Where does Marc go to escape the rain?
What does Marc order inside?
How does Marc get home dry?
choices: He waits for the rain to stop
A woman lends him her umbrella

segments.fr: C'est l'anniversaire de Sophie, et sa mère prépare un gâteau.
segments.en: It's Sophie's birthday, and her mother is making a cake.
segments.grammarNote: 'C'est' introduces a fact or situation, very common in spoken French.

segments.fr: Elle suit une vieille recette écrite par sa grand-mère.
segments.en: She follows an old recipe written by her grandmother.
segments.grammarNote: 'Écrite' agrees in gender with 'recette' (feminine).

segments.fr: Malheureusement, elle oublie le sucre dans la pâte.
segments.en: Unfortunately, she forgets the sugar in the batter.
segments.grammarNote: 'Malheureusement' is an adverb built from 'malheureux' + '-ment'.

segments.fr: Le gâteau sort du four, mais il n'est pas du tout sucré.
segments.en: The cake comes out of the oven, but it isn't sweet at all.
segments.grammarNote: 'Pas du tout' strongly negates, stronger than plain 'pas'.

segments.fr: Sophie rit et décide d'ajouter beaucoup de glaçage.
segments.en: Sophie laughs and decides to add lots of icing.
segments.grammarNote: 'Décider de' + infinitive is how 'to decide to' is formed.

segments.fr: Finalement, tout le monde adore ce gâteau un peu spécial.
segments.en: In the end, everyone loves this slightly special cake.
segments.grammarNote: 'Tout le monde' is singular in French even though it means 'everyone'.

quiz.q: What is Sophie's mother making?
Whose recipe does she follow?
What mistake does she make?
How does Sophie fix the cake?

segments.fr: Amadou attend son train sur le quai de la gare.
segments.en: Amadou is waiting for his train on the station platform.
segments.grammarNote: 'Attendre' means 'to wait for' and needs no extra preposition.

segments.fr: Une annonce dit que le train a vingt minutes de retard.
segments.en: An announcement says the train is twenty minutes late.
segments.grammarNote: 'Avoir du retard' is how French expresses being late for transport.

segments.fr: Amadou décide d'acheter un café pour patienter.
segments.en: Amadou decides to buy a coffee to pass the time.
segments.grammarNote: 'Patienter' means 'to wait patiently', a bit formal.

segments.fr: Il discute avec un autre voyageur qui va dans la même ville.
segments.en: He chats with another traveller who is going to the same city.
segments.grammarNote: 'Même' before a noun means 'same'; after it means 'itself'.

segments.fr: Le train arrive enfin, et tout le monde monte rapidement.
segments.en: The train finally arrives, and everyone gets on quickly.
segments.grammarNote: 'Enfin' means 'finally', showing relief after waiting.

segments.fr: Amadou et son nouvel ami s'assoient ensemble pendant le trajet.
segments.en: Amadou and his new friend sit together during the trip.
segments.grammarNote: 'S'asseoir' is reflexive and irregular; 's'assoient' is its 'ils/elles' form.

quiz.q: Where is Amadou waiting?
How late is the train?
What does Amadou buy while waiting?
What happens on the train?
choices: He sits with his new friend

segments.fr: Un nouveau voisin emménage dans l'appartement d'à côté.
segments.en: A new neighbour is moving into the apartment next door.
segments.grammarNote: 'Emménager' means 'to move in'; 'déménager' means 'to move out'.
segments.pronunciationTip: 'Emménage' and 'déménage' sound almost identical, listen for the first syllable.

segments.fr: Il porte des boîtes toute la journée sans s'arrêter.
segments.en: He carries boxes all day long without stopping.
segments.grammarNote: 'Sans s'arrêter' uses a reflexive infinitive after 'sans'.

segments.fr: Le soir, il joue de la guitare, un peu trop fort.
segments.en: In the evening, he plays the guitar, a bit too loudly.
segments.grammarNote: 'Jouer de' is used for musical instruments; 'jouer à' for games and sports.

segments.fr: Sa voisine frappe à la porte pour se plaindre poliment.
segments.en: His neighbour knocks on the door to complain politely.
segments.grammarNote: 'Se plaindre' is reflexive and means 'to complain'.

segments.fr: Le nouveau voisin s'excuse et lui offre une part de pizza.
segments.en: The new neighbour apologizes and offers her a slice of pizza.
segments.grammarNote: 'S'excuser' is reflexive: literally 'to excuse oneself'.

segments.fr: Depuis ce jour-là, ils sont devenus de bons amis.
segments.en: Since that day, they have become good friends.
segments.grammarNote: 'Depuis' + a point in time means 'since', showing a lasting result.

quiz.q: What is the new neighbour doing all day?
What does he do in the evening that bothers her?
choices: He plays loud music on speakers

quiz.q: Why does the neighbour knock on his door?
How does the story end?
